diagrams.title: DFA State Diagram - Basic Structure
diagrams.description: A generic DFA state diagram showing the fundamental components: states (circles), transitions (arrows with input symbols), start state (arrow pointing to it), and accept/final states (double circles). This visual structure is essential for understanding how DFAs process input strings.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the visual representation and components of a DFA state diagram, the primary way DFAs are visualized in Theory of Computation

diagrams.title: DFA Example: Binary Strings Ending with '1'
diagrams.description: A concrete DFA example that accepts binary strings ending with '1'. Shows two states - q0 (not in accept, representing last symbol was 0) and q1 (accept state, representing last symbol was 1), with transitions on input symbols '0' and '1'. This demonstrates how DFAs use states to 'remember' relevant information about processed input.
elements: self-loop on '0' from q0 to q0
transition from q0 to q1 on '1'
self-loop on '1' from q1 to q1
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates a practical DFA example showing how states track memory of the last input symbol to determine string acceptance

diagrams.title: DFA Formal Definition - 5-Tuple Notation
diagrams.description: The formal mathematical definition of a DFA shown as a 5-tuple M = (Q, Σ, δ, q0, F), where Q is the finite set of states, Σ is the input alphabet, δ is the transition function, q0 is the start state, and F is the set of accept states. This connects the visual state diagram to its mathematical formalization.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the mathematical formalization of DFAs using the 5-tuple notation, bridging visual diagrams with formal Theory of Computation definitions
